name: mammoth.js (DOCX to HTML Converter)

performance.notes: Parsing-bound; proportional to document complexity and size

agent_readiness.notes: Convert DOCX to clean HTML or Markdown. const mammoth = require('mammoth'). mammoth.convertToHtml({path: 'document.docx'}).then(result => { const html = result.value; const messages = result.messages; }). mammoth.convertToMarkdown({path: 'doc.docx'}). mammoth.extractRawText({path: 'doc.docx'}). Buffer: mammoth.convertToHtml({buffer: docxBuffer}). Style mapping: mammoth.convertToHtml({path}, { styleMap: ['p[style-name=\'Heading 1\'] => h1:fresh', 'p[style-name=\'Quote\'] => blockquote'] }). Image handling: convert to base64 or custom handler. result.messages for warnings. Python package: mammoth (same author, similar API).

gotchas: Complex DOCX formatting (tables with merged cells, SmartArt) may convert poorly
Images convert to base64 by default — large files bloat HTML; use custom image handler
Style mapping requires knowledge of source DOCX style names
result.messages contains warnings — check for conversion issues
best_when: Converting Word documents to HTML for CMS or web display
Extracting text content from DOCX for indexing or processing
Migrating Word-based content to web-first formats
avoid_when: When pixel-perfect formatting preservation is required (use LibreOffice headless)
summary: mammoth.js converts DOCX to clean HTML or Markdown. mammoth.convertToHtml({path}).then(result => result.value). convertToMarkdown(). extractRawText(). Style mapping for custom HTML output. Image base64 or custom handler. BSD-2 by Michael Williamson. Type-annotated. Best for DOCX → HTML migration and text extraction. Complex formatting may convert poorly. Check result.messages for warnings. Also available as Python package.
